docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: This site is maintained by volunteers. To document a process or fix something out of date, reach out to the tech team at webdev@spokanemountaineers.org.

docs.title: About This Documentation
docs.text:
Welcome to the official documentation site for the Spokane Mountaineers — a nonprofit community of adventurers, educators, and volunteers dedicated to promoting safe and inclusive outdoor recreation since 1915.

This site is maintained by club members to provide:

  • How-to guides for managing club activities and technology
  • Administrative tools and resources for leaders and volunteers
  • Best practices for website management, event registration, and communications

docs.title: Who We Are
docs.text:
The Spokane Mountaineers is an all-volunteer organization committed to the responsible enjoyment of the outdoors. We offer year-round activities in:

  • Mountaineering
  • Rock climbing
  • Backcountry skiing
  • Hiking and backpacking
  • Mountain biking
  • Conservation and stewardship

Our members range from first-time hikers to seasoned climbers, all sharing a passion for learning and the natural world.

docs.title: How This Site Works
docs.text:
This site is built with MkDocs and the Material for MkDocs theme. It lives on GitHub and is open to contributions from any club member.

Want to contribute? Start here:

  • Visit the GitHub repository
  • Submit an issue or request a guide

docs.title: Thanks
docs.text:
Special thanks to all our volunteers, instructors, and behind-the-scenes helpers who make the Spokane Mountaineers thrive — on the trails and online.

“The mountains are calling and I must go.” — John Muir

docs.title: Nonprofit Programs & Affiliations
docs.text:
The Spokane Mountaineers is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization. As such, we are eligible for various nonprofit programs and grants that provide us with essential tools and infrastructure at reduced or no cost.

This page tracks our current affiliations, the benefits we receive, and the status of pending applications.

docs.title: Summary of Active Programs
docs.text: Organization Program Status Primary Benefit Google Google for Nonprofits Active Google Workspace (Free) Microsoft Microsoft for Nonprofits Active $2,000 Annual Azure Grant Slack Slack for Nonprofits Pending Free Pro Plan (Expected) TechSoup Validation Services Active Eligibility Verification

docs.title: Google for Nonprofits
docs.text:
Google provides us with a full suite of productivity tools that power our official communications and document storage.

  • Benefits:
    • Google Workspace: Custom email addresses (@spokanemountaineers.org) for board members and committees.
    • Shared Drives: Centralized storage for club records and assets.
  • Administration: Managed via the Google Admin Console.
  • Key Contact: Web Development Team (webdev@spokanemountaineers.org).

docs.title: Microsoft for Nonprofits
docs.text:
Microsoft offers a variety of grants and discounted software to help nonprofits scale their impact.

  • Benefits:
    • Azure Grant: We receive a $2,000 annual credit for Azure services. This is currently used to host our website and related infrastructure.
    • Microsoft 365: Eligibility for donated or heavily discounted M365 Business Premium licenses.
  • Administration: Managed via the Microsoft Nonprofit Portal.
  • Usage: Credits are applied to our Azure Subscription. Monitoring is required to ensure we stay within the grant limit.

docs.title: Slack for Nonprofits
docs.text:
We are currently in the process of registering for Slack's nonprofit program to improve internal club communication.

  • Target Benefits:
    • Free upgrade to the Pro Plan for workspaces with fewer than 250 members.
    • Unlimited message history and integrations.
  • Status: Application in progress. Requires TechSoup validation.

docs.title: TechSoup
docs.text:
TechSoup is the primary validation service used by almost all major technology providers (Google, Microsoft, Slack, etc.) to verify nonprofit status.

  • Validation Token: Often required when applying for new programs.
  • Account Access: Access is limited to the Treasurer and the Web Development lead.

docs.title: Required Documentation
docs.text:
When applying for new programs, the following documents are typically required:

  1. IRS Determination Letter: Confirming our 501(c)(3) status.
  2. Form 990: Our most recent tax filing.
  3. Bylaws: Occasionally requested to verify organizational structure.

docs.title: Technical Articles & Solutions
docs.text: This section contains in-depth technical articles and solutions we've implemented for the Spokane Mountaineers. Unlike step-by-step guides, these articles provide detailed explanations of complex problems, architectural decisions, and innovative solutions.

docs.title: Recent Articles
docs.text:
• Migrating to Email-Based Login – How we built custom login and password reset pages that automatically append .smi to member email addresses, allowing members to login using just their email while maintaining Salesforce's global username uniqueness requirement.
  • Fixing Missing Approval Email Notifications – How we diagnosed and fixed a Salesforce platform behavior change that stopped approval emails from being sent to activity group chairs.
  • Restricting Member Access to Account Records in Experience Cloud – How we resolved a privacy concern by removing Account object read access for member-facing profiles, protecting sensitive financial and household data.
  • Event Participant Follow Handler: Tests, Deployment, and Lessons Learned – End-to-end recap of the latest handler improvements, unit testing strategy, and deployment verification steps.
  • Protecting Member Privacy in Event Participant Lists – An overview of our privacy solution for Event Participants, written for a general technical audience.
  • Event Participant Privacy Redirect Solution (Technical) – Complete technical implementation guide with deployment instructions, code examples, and troubleshooting.

Member sign-in help (Google and Microsoft) now lives under For Members. The technical OAuth write-ups are in Automation.
